# UC-19.1.36 · Service Profile vNIC Redundancy and Failover Audit

## Description

A service profile with a single active vNIC or mismatched failover policy removes network redundancy for VMs. Auditing template-derived settings reduces surprise outages during single-path failures.

## Value

A service profile with a single active vNIC or mismatched failover policy removes network redundancy for VMs. Auditing template-derived settings reduces surprise outages during single-path failures.

## Implementation

(1) Poll vNIC objects for each production service profile; (2) flag profiles with fewer than two data vNICs or empty redundancy pair; (3) integrate with CMDB to exclude intentionally single-NIC appliance profiles via lookup.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cisco_ucs` `sourcetype="cisco:ucs:config"` with fields `sp_name`, `vnic_name`, `redundancy_pair`, `peer_vnic`, `switch_id`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Poll vNIC objects for each production service profile; (2) flag profiles with fewer than two data vNICs or empty redundancy pair; (3) integrate with CMDB to exclude intentionally single-NIC appliance profiles via lookup.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:config" object_type="vnic" earliest=-24h
| stats dc(vnic_name) as vnic_count, values(redundancy_pair) as pairs by sp_name
| eval has_pair=if(vnic_count>=2 OR mvcount(pairs)>0, "Yes", "No")
| where has_pair=="No"
| table sp_name, vnic_count, pairs, has_pair
```

Understanding this SPL

**Service Profile vNIC Redundancy and Failover Audit** — A service profile with a single active vNIC or mismatched failover policy removes network redundancy for VMs. Auditing template-derived settings reduces surprise outages during single-path failures.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cisco_ucs` `sourcetype="cisco:ucs:config"` with fields `sp_name`, `vnic_name`, `redundancy_pair`, `peer_vnic`, `switch_id`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_ucs; **sourcetype**: cisco:ucs:config. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_ucs, sourcetype="cisco:ucs:config", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sp_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **has_pair** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where has_pair=="No"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Service Profile vNIC Redundancy and Failover Audit**): table sp_name, vnic_count, pairs, has_pair


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco UCS Manager (faults, equipment, and service profile context) for the same DNs and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (profiles lacking redundancy), Pie chart (redundant vs single-path), Bar chart (by org).

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:config" object_type="vnic" earliest=-24h
| stats dc(vnic_name) as vnic_count, values(redundancy_pair) as pairs by sp_name
| eval has_pair=if(vnic_count>=2 OR mvcount(pairs)>0, "Yes", "No")
| where has_pair=="No"
| table sp_name, vnic_count, pairs, has_pair
```

## Visualization

Table (profiles lacking redundancy), Pie chart (redundant vs single-path), Bar chart (by org).
